channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 10 Anson Rd, 19th April
item.description:
Fly tipping and large sofa dumped on the street. Pls come to remove the items and sweep the street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Anson Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Neglected and unpleasant !, 19th April
item.description:
I would like to raise a concern regarding the condition of the area surrounding the library by the Mountview building next to bus stop C on Peckham Hill Street. The space is constantly, on daily basis very neglected with a significant amount of random rubbish scattered around. This not only creates an unpleasant environment for visitors but also reflects poorly on the upkeep of a public community space. Additionally, the green areas and plant life appear to be poorly maintained. This space is clearly intended to provide a pleasant, green environment for the community, but it is not being properly cared With spring approaching, this would be an ideal time for the council to take action to restore and maintain the area. As this falls under the responsibility of Southwark Council, I would strongly urge more regular patrols, cleaning, and proper maintenance of the greenery to ensure the area is kept clean, safe, and welcoming tor everyone. Furthermore, appropriate warning signs should be installed to discourage antisocial behaviour such as spitting , littering and drinking alcohol. Littering is ILLEGAL in the UK and is a criminal offence under Section 87 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990. These signs should clearly state that such actions are not permitted and that penalties may be issued to those who fail to comply with the rules. I hope this issue can be addressed promptly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 120, Peckham Hill Street, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massive sewage leak neglected for a very long time and still spilling to the surface. Not good for our health. We need urgent help., 19th April
item.description:
Blocked manhole and massive sewage spilling on surface without any attention. This has been on for a while and is at a dangerous point. Already a threat to health.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): De Beauvoir Estate, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Plants in road, 19th April
item.description:
These children have removed & thrown more than 10 plants from the bank & thrown them into the roaf

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albion Road, Blendon, Greater London

item.title: Overflowing recycling bins, 19th April
item.description:
Looks like they haven’t been emptied in weeks. Cardboard and other rubbish everywhere including food items. Rats are present - have seen them running through the cardboard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big box of rubbish, 19th April
item.description:
Opposite Dulwich medical centre

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 146, Crystal Palace Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars and vans being left on street no tax, 19th April
item.description:
This van has been outside my house for two weeks now. There is constant cars and vans brought from no 286. Iv reported this problem loads of times and nothing seems to be done. He buys old cars and vans from auction and sometimes there’s 6-8 cars/ vans out the front and there is literally no place to park. All the vehicles are untaxed and most have no mot too. Something needs to be done about this

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 276, High Street, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cafe under Kentish Town west station, 19th April
item.description:
Cafe fills the pavement with furniture and cabinets so difficult to walk past
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Prince of Wales Road, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elephant and Castle, Greater London

item.title: Timing of emptying bins, 19th April
item.description:
The bins at the junction of Myddleton Avenue/Heron Drive are regularly full to overflowing. The schedule of emptying needs to be reviewed. The bins have been in their current state since Friday am. A full bag of litter has not been collected for 2.5 days.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Myddleton Avenue, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Please clean that area and provide bins and signs NoLittering Here, 19th April
item.description:
The area between Mountview and the bus stop C is in absolute state every single day. It is adjacent to the library, Mountview and it should be clean and pleasent.Litter picking and street sweeping should be done at least once daily and the plants should be attend to as well, there are objects being thrown in there. People throw rubbish all over the place so clear signs with possible fine info should be added to educate.More bins should be provided as well. Please assist.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 120, Peckham Hill Street, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
